chunk_summary: This section outlines the minimum documentation, equipment, and staffing requirements for Indian milk banks, emphasizing safety, traceability, and compliance with national standards.
learning_objectives: Understand the essential documentation required for milk bank operations in India.
Identify the minimum equipment necessary for safe milk processing and storage.
Recognize the key staffing roles and their responsibilities in milk banks.
Learn how to align milk bank practices with national quality assurance standards.
key_facts: Minimum documentation includes donor registration, serology reports, pasteurization records, and refrigerator/freezer temperature logs.
Essential equipment includes validated pasteurization systems, freezers, refrigerators, and insulated transport boxes for high ambient temperatures.
Staffing must include a clinical lead, trained lactation counselors, milk bank technicians, and microbiology support.
Traceability and cold-chain control are critical for safe milk bank operations.
Internal audits and periodic traceability exercises strengthen compliance with NQAS.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: An India-facing 'minimum package' clarifies what is non-negotiable for safe milk bank services and what can be adapted locally. (Source: National Quality Assurance Standards CLMC module)
document_key_topics: Staffing roles and responsibilities in milk banks
